title: Daily Financial Briefing
marketTone: CPI miss eases July-hike panic, but Warsh says not 'mission accomplished' — S&P +0.4% and Nasdaq +0.9% close up while Brent settles at $84.73 and Iran blockade risk persists.
summary: June CPI downside surprise (BLS, 8:30am ET): headline -0.4% m/m, 3.5% y/y (vs ~3.8% consensus, 4.2% May); core flat m/m, 2.6% y/y (vs ~0.2% m/m and ~2.9% y/y consensus); energy -5.7% m/m, shelter +0.1% — largest monthly headline drop since April 2020.

Post-CPI tape: 10y Treasury fell ~6bp to ~4.57%; CME FedWatch July hike odds fell to ~16.6% (hold ~83.4%); S&P closed +0.40% at 7,545.13, Nasdaq +0.92% at 26,110.62; COMEX gold settled +1.60% at $4,061.10/oz (Morningstar/Dow Jones).

Geopolitics/oil: Brent settled +1.72% at $84.73/bbl, WTI +1.54% at $79.34 (CNBC/Morningstar); Trump scrapped the 20% Hormuz fee but reinstated Iran-only blockade; Warsh House testimony stressed price stability, no forward guidance, and June CPI is not 'mission accomplished' (Fed/CNN).
signal: Core disinflation landed — but Warsh and still-rising oil tell markets the June print is backward-looking; the Fed path hinges on July energy pass-through and Wednesday PPI, not one soft CPI alone.
watch: Wednesday PPI (Jul 15) and Warsh Senate testimony — does upstream inflation re-heat the hawkish narrative Warsh kept alive despite the CPI miss?
globalRegime: The Fed holds at 3.50%–3.75%. June CPI delivered a broad miss vs consensus — headline 3.5% y/y (vs ~3.8%), core 2.6% y/y (vs ~2.9%) — pulling July hike odds from ~45–50% pre-data to ~16.6% post-data (CME FedWatch via BlockBeats). Chair Warsh's first Humphrey-Hawkins hearing reaffirmed zero tolerance for elevated inflation and pledged no forward guidance, while telling lawmakers June's drop is not 'mission accomplished' given this week's oil spike. The July Monetary Policy Report still says inflation 'stepped up' this spring.
globalChanged: June CPI miss across the board: -0.4% m/m headline (vs -0.1% to -0.2% expected), 3.5% y/y (vs ~3.8%); core flat m/m (vs +0.2%), 2.6% y/y (vs ~2.9%); shelter +0.1%; energy -5.7% m/m but +15.7% y/y (BLS; consensus CNBC/Reuters).

Post-CPI risk-on close: S&P +0.40% to 7,545.13, Nasdaq +0.92% to 26,110.62, Dow +0.08% to 52,538.58; 10y yield ~4.57% (-~6bp); July Fed hike probability ~16.6% vs ~45–50% Monday (CME FedWatch; Reuters/MarketScreener).

Oil premium intact despite fee U-turn: Brent settled $84.73 (+1.72%), WTI $79.34 (+1.54%); Trump dropped the 20% Hormuz cargo fee for Gulf investment deals but kept Iran-only naval blockade (AP/BBC); intraday Brent touched >$86 before settling lower (CBS).

Warsh debut + bank earnings: prepared remarks stress restoring price stability; Q&A rejects 'mission accomplished' on CPI; JPM Q2 NI $21.2bn ($7.70 EPS, +41% y/y), Citi $5.8bn ($3.15 EPS, +45% y/y) — both beat on trading/IB strength (SEC filings).
globalImplies: July FOMC hold is now the base case (~83% per CME), but September hike pricing near ~60% (Reuters/FXStreet) means one CPI miss did not kill the tightening narrative.
June gasoline-driven relief predates this week's >9% oil spike (Mon) and fresh Hormuz escalation; July CPI will capture forward energy pressure.
Strong bank earnings support the 'solid economy' framing in Warsh's MPR, giving the Fed room to stay hawkish rhetorically even if July hike odds collapsed.
globalTensions: CPI and oil still tell opposite stories — June headline fell on energy that has since surged, and Warsh explicitly warned against declaring victory. Equities closed higher on CPI relief while Brent sits at a one-month high settle; gold rebounded to $4,061 COMEX settle (+1.6%) as yields fell, but the inflation-oil overhang limits the rally's durability.
chinaStance: Moderately loose via quantity, cautious on price — PBOC net-injected CNY226.5bn via 7-day OMO Tuesday (236.5bn ops − 10bn maturity) and announced a CNY1.4tn 6-month buyout reverse repo for Jul 15; 7-day rate held at 1.40%, no LPR-cut signal while oil lifts imported-inflation risk (PBOC/Xinhua Finance).
chinaChanged: 华尔街见闻早餐 (Jul 14): overnight Mon US tape — Nasdaq -1.55% to 25,873, SOX -4.78%, SK Hynix Seoul -15.37%; spot gold ~$3,999 (-2.93%); Brent intraday ~+10% to $83.30 settle; BTC >3% lower, briefly <$62k — oil→Fed chain, not classic safe-haven (华尔街见闻).
A-shares V-recovery Tue: Shanghai +1.36% to 3,967.13, Shenzhen +2.77%, ChiNext +3.43%, CSI 300 +0.92% to 4,738; >4,200 advancers; ~CNY2.7tn turnover — PCB/hardware and oil/gas led (Xinhua/新华财经).

BlockBeats/律动 (Jul 14): CPI miss — headline 3.5% y/y vs 3.8% expected, core flat m/m; post-data July hike odds fell to 16.6% (hold 83.4%, CME FedWatch); Trump replaced 20% Hormuz fee with Gulf investment deals, strait open except Iran (BlockBeats flash 356087/356124/356151).

PBOC liquidity stack: Tue CNY236.5bn 7-day reverse repos at 1.40% (net +CNY226.5bn after CNY10bn maturity); Jul 15 CNY1.4tn 6-month buyout RR announced (expire 2027-01-15) — mid-tenor cushion into tax week Jul 15–17 (PBOC/中国金融信息网).
华尔街见闻 policy color: Premier Li Qiang stressed smooth old/new growth-engine transition; 《扩大消费“十五五”规划》 targets social retail sales ~CNY60tn by 2030 with an 'AI+consumption' push (华尔街见闻早餐).
chinaImplies: CNY1.4tn buyout RR cushions Jul 15–17 tax-period funding stress, but does not neutralize oil-driven PPI/import-bill risk — quantity easing ≠ rate pivot.
If CPI relief sticks and Warsh's Senate tone stays non-committal on near-term hikes, A-share hardware bounce can extend; re-hawkish Warsh + Brent >$84 keeps ChiNext fragile.
BlockBeats frames BTC as a high-beta liquidity asset in the oil→Fed chain — CPI miss helped odds, but Hormuz blockade risk keeps crypto tied to macro rates, not digital-gold status.
chinaDivergences: Onshore equities staged a V-recovery Tuesday while 华尔街见闻's overnight book showed US tech/semis, gold, and BTC all weak with oil surging — China price action and global risk assets remain desynchronized. Nikkei +0.74% to 67,743.50 and Kospi +0.73% to 6,856.83 closed firmer after Monday's sell-sidecar, but China semis materials still lagged hardware leadership.

assetFramework.regime: Fed path via real yields — CPI-vs-oil tug decides the discount rate; earnings a secondary support
assetFramework.driver: 10y -6bp to ~4.57% post-CPI miss; July hike odds ~16.6% vs ~45–50% Monday (CME FedWatch); JPM/Citi Q2 beats
assetFramework.read: S&P +0.40% to 7,545, Nasdaq +0.92% — consistent with the rates-relief beta; bank earnings added support, not the driver
assetFramework.invalidator: Equities fall while yields also fall for 2+ sessions — would signal earnings/growth fear replacing the rates regime

assetFramework.regime: Inflation prints vs consensus + Fed communication — data-led range trading
assetFramework.driver: June CPI miss (3.5% y/y vs ~3.8%); Warsh: no forward guidance, not 'mission accomplished'
assetFramework.read: 10y ~4.57% (-6bp) — CPI beta dominated; Warsh's hawkish frame limited the rally rather than reversing it
assetFramework.invalidator: 10y back above 4.65% without a hot data print — would signal fiscal-supply or term-premium regime taking over

assetFramework.regime: Rate differentials with a safe-haven bid on Hormuz headlines
assetFramework.driver: July hike odds collapsed post-CPI; blockade risk still live
assetFramework.read: Softer rates pull vs geopolitical bid roughly offset — no decisive move; regime intact
assetFramework.invalidator: DXY breaks lower while September hike odds hold ~60% — would suggest flows, not rates, in control

assetFramework.regime: Two-layer: classic real-yield beta day-to-day, central-bank/fiscal-hedge floor structurally (post-2022 regime)
assetFramework.driver: Settled +1.60% at $4,061 as 10y fell ~6bp; Mon it fell ~2.6% when yields rose
assetFramework.read: Trading the real-yield beta cleanly this week; the CB-buying floor is why dips keep getting bought
assetFramework.invalidator: Gold falls >3% on a day real yields decline — would question the hedge-demand floor

assetFramework.regime: Geopolitical supply risk — Hormuz blockade premium dominates demand signals
assetFramework.driver: Brent settled $84.73 (+1.72%) after Mon +9.6%; Trump kept Iran-only blockade, dropped 20% fee
assetFramework.read: Premium persists despite fee U-turn — supply-risk beta, not demand, is pricing the barrel
assetFramework.invalidator: Brent settles below $78 with Hormuz crossings >40/day — premium unwound

assetFramework.regime: Domestic liquidity + positioning snapback; partially decoupled from global risk beta
assetFramework.driver: PBOC net +CNY226.5bn OMO, CNY1.4tn buyout RR for Jul 15; Mon -2.06% washout
assetFramework.read: V-recovery (+1.36% / +3.43%) on liquidity + oversold bounce while US semis fell — domestic beta won today
assetFramework.invalidator: Shanghai closes below Monday's low on rising volume — snapback thesis dead, global risk beta back in charge

assetFramework.regime: PBOC fix as the policy signal; rate differentials constrain but do not drive daily moves
assetFramework.driver: 7-day OMO rate held at 1.40%; quantity easing without price easing
assetFramework.read: Stable-fix regime — PBOC is signaling FX stability while easing via quantity; imported energy inflation argues against rate cuts
assetFramework.invalidator: Fix persistently weaker than model estimates for a week — would signal tolerated depreciation

assetFramework.regime: High-beta global liquidity asset in the oil→Fed chain — not digital gold in this shock
assetFramework.driver: Mon: BTC >3% down, briefly <$62k with SOX -4.78%; post-CPI rebound toward $63.5k as hike odds fell (BlockBeats)
assetFramework.read: Textbook liquidity beta both directions — sold the hawkish repricing, bought the CPI relief
assetFramework.invalidator: BTC rallies while Brent >$84 and hike odds rise — would signal decoupling from the rates chain

signals.name: Core disinflation pivot — June CPI miss
signals.evidence: Core flat m/m, 2.6% y/y (vs ~2.9% expected); headline -0.4% m/m, 3.5% y/y (vs ~3.8%); shelter +0.1%; July hike odds ~16.6% post-data (BLS; CME FedWatch/BlockBeats).
signals.mechanism: Broad downside surprise vs consensus eases near-term re-hike urgency and supports duration/risk assets — unless Fed officials dismiss it as backward-looking.
signals.disprovedIf: September hike probability reclaims >70% and 10y yields break above 4.65% within one week.

signals.name: Hormuz blockade premium persists despite fee reversal
signals.evidence: Brent settled $84.73 (+1.72%), WTI $79.34; Mon +9.4%/+9.6% closes; Trump scrapped 20% fee but kept Iran blockade; BlockBeats/Trump post Jul 14 (Reuters, CNBC, BlockBeats 356124).
signals.mechanism: Supply-risk and shipping disruption raise forward inflation expectations, limiting how much June CPI relief can reprice Fed path.
signals.disprovedIf: Brent settles below $78 for three sessions and daily Hormuz crossings exceed 40 without new escalation.

signals.name: Warsh hawkish frame survives CPI miss
signals.evidence: Prepared remarks: 'no tolerance' for elevated inflation; Q&A: June drop not 'mission accomplished'; no forward guidance; oil >$84 settle (Fed/CNN/BlockBeats 356132).
signals.mechanism: Chair sets interpretive filter — markets must weigh backward-looking CPI against forward oil shock; keeps September hike pricing alive (~60% per FXStreet).
signals.disprovedIf: Warsh Senate testimony Wed explicitly downplays September hike risk and cites June CPI as sufficient progress.

signals.name: China onshore rebound is tactical, not a policy pivot
signals.evidence: Shanghai +1.36%, ChiNext +3.43% with hardware/oil leadership after Mon -2.06% (华尔街见闻); PBOC net +CNY226.5bn OMO plus CNY1.4tn buyout RR for Jul 15 (PBOC).
signals.mechanism: Liquidity quantity + positioning snapback, not an LPR/rate pivot — oil import inflation still constrains price tools.
signals.disprovedIf: Shanghai closes below Monday's low on rising volume with sustained northbound outflows.

signals.name: Oil→Fed chain hits crypto as risk asset
signals.evidence: 华尔街见闻: BTC >3% down Mon, briefly <$62k with SOX -4.78%; BlockBeats: July hike odds 16.6% post-CPI vs ~50% pre-data; BTC rebounded toward $63.5k+ after CPI (BlockBeats 356087/356151).
signals.mechanism: Energy shock → inflation/Fed-path anxiety → de-risking of duration and crypto; CPI miss provides relief but does not decouple BTC from rates if oil stays elevated.
signals.disprovedIf: BTC reclaims pre-escalation levels while Brent stays above $84 for two sessions with stable Fed odds.

signals.name: Backward-looking CPI may be a head-fake
signals.evidence: June energy -5.7% m/m on gasoline relief, but spot oil spiked >9% Mon and Brent settled $84.73 Tue; CPI measurement period predates Hormuz re-escalation.
signals.mechanism: Markets may cheer the print then refocus on July energy pass-through and Wednesday PPI.
signals.disprovedIf: PPI core comes in below consensus and equities sustain gains through Warsh Senate testimony.

watchItems.headline: Wednesday PPI + Warsh Senate testimony
watchItems.why: STRONG CPI-disinflation signal meets STRONG Hormuz premium; upstream prices and Warsh's Day-2 tone decide whether hawkish pricing returns.
watchItems.watch: US June PPI (Jul 15, 8:30am ET); Senate Banking Committee hearing; language on June CPI vs oil, 'policy firming,' September path.
watchItems.trigger: Core PPI ≥0.3% m/m and Warsh endorses near-term tightening despite CPI miss.
watchItems.invalidator: Core PPI ≤0.1% m/m and Warsh downplays September hike risk.

watchItems.headline: Hormuz blockade vs. oil settle band
watchItems.why: STRONG energy-premium signal; forward inflation risk that can overwrite June CPI.
watchItems.watch: Brent/WTI daily settle; CENTCOM/US statements; ship-tracking vessel counts.
watchItems.trigger: Brent sustains above $85 on settle for 3 sessions or crossings stay below 20/day.
watchItems.invalidator: Brent settle below $80 with crossings >40/day and no new military strikes for 5 days.

watchItems.headline: Post-CPI yield and equity follow-through
watchItems.why: STRONG core-disinflation signal; tests whether CPI relief sticks against oil overhang.
watchItems.watch: 10y Treasury yield vs ~4.57% post-CPI; S&P holding 7,545 close; semis recovery after Mon SOX -4.78%.
watchItems.trigger: 10y falls below 4.50% and S&P reclaims 7,575 (Jul 10 high) on volume.
watchItems.invalidator: 10y reclaims 4.65% and S&P fails to hold 7,500 by Thursday close.

watchItems.headline: PBOC CNY1.4tn buyout reverse repo (Jul 15)
watchItems.why: MODERATE China liquidity signal; tests whether quantity tools keep funding calm through tax week.
watchItems.watch: Jul 15 operation results; DR007 vs 1.40% policy rate; follow-on OMO Jul 15–17.
watchItems.trigger: DR007 rises above 1.50% for two sessions despite the CNY1.4tn injection.
watchItems.invalidator: DR007 stays ≤1.40% through Jul 17 with orderly funding.

watchItems.headline: ASML/TSMC earnings (Jul 15–16)
watchItems.why: MODERATE semi-cycle signal; capex guidance tests AI narrative after Mon's chip selloff.
watchItems.watch: ASML bookings (Jul 15), TSMC revenue/capex guide (Jul 16); SOX vs Mon -4.78%.
watchItems.trigger: Both guide below consensus and Nasdaq semis fail to reclaim Monday losses.
watchItems.invalidator: ASML bookings beat and TSMC capex guide above street; SOX +3% from Mon close.

sources: Bureau of Labor Statistics (June CPI), Federal Reserve (Warsh testimony Jul 14; July MPR), CME FedWatch, Reuters, CNBC, AP, BBC, Morningstar/Dow Jones, SEC (JPM/Citi Q2), 华尔街见闻 (breakfast 2026-07-14), BlockBeats/律动 (flash 356087/356124/356132/356151), Xinhua/新华财经, PBOC/中国金融信息网, Mainichi/Businesskorea (Nikkei/Kospi closes).
singleSource: IBM -22% profit-warning drag on Dow (AP/QZ); Kpler Hormuz crossing counts via prior-day CNBC; Xingzheng year-line support thesis via 华尔街见闻.
